seo.title: LangGraph Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangGraph agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using LangGraph build complex agent workflows for personalized recommendations, dynamic pricing, and customer support, but common failure modes like cyclic executions and state corruption can lead to inaccurate product claims, pricing errors, or policy misrepresentations. These issues risk FTC enforcement, CCPA/GDPR violations, PCI DSS non-compliance, and ADA accessibility failures. Vex provides essential guardrails by verifying state integrity and outputs against compliance rules at every graph transition.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims in recommendations or ads must be substantiated with evidence, avoiding deceptive marketing practices.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies recommendation outputs against a knowledge base of substantiated claims, blocking unsubstantiated suggestions before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data processing in personalization workflows must respect consent and provide opt-out mechanisms.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data minimization by validating state transitions only include consented data fields, preventing unauthorized PII propagation.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment workflows must securely handle card data without logging sensitive information in checkpoints.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans graph state for PCI-sensitive data leakage and redacts it from checkpoints and logs automatically.

complianceRequirements.description: Dynamic content generation must produce accessible HTML compliant with WCAG 2.1 standards.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates generated product descriptions and emails against accessibility rules, ensuring screen-reader compatibility.

industryRisks.name: Cyclic Pricing Updates
industryRisks.description: Infinite loops in dynamic pricing graphs cause runaway price changes, violating accurate pricing regulations.
industryRisks.example: Pricing agent cycles between discount and surge logic, displaying $99 → $199 → $49 prices to customers.

industryRisks.name: State Corruption in Cart Merge
industryRisks.description: Parallel recommendation branches overwrite discount codes, leading to refund disputes.
industryRisks.example: Guest cart merges with loyalty cart, losing 20% discount code and overcharging returning customers.

industryRisks.name: Checkpoint Schema Drift
industryRisks.description: Deserialization failures after adding GDPR consent fields crash personalization workflows.
industryRisks.example: Post-update, old checkpoints fail to load, serving generic recommendations instead of personalized ones.

industryRisks.name: Bypassed Refund Policy Check
industryRisks.description: Conditional edges skip human-in-loop verification, misrepresenting return policies.
industryRisks.example: Automated agent promises 90-day returns instead of 30-day policy, triggering FTC complaints.

implementation.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect against cyclic execution and state corruption by verifying inputs/outputs on every invocation.
implementation.code:
import vex
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

# Your LangGraph state
def check_pricing_accuracy(state):
    if state['price'] < 0 or state['price'] > 10000:
        raise ValueError('Invalid pricing')
    return state

# Vex verification
def verify_ecommerce_state(state):
    with vex.verify(policy='ecommerce-pricing') as v:
        v.expect(state['product_claims']).to_be_substantiated()
        v.expect(state['price']).to_be_accurate()
        v.expect(state['refund_policy']).to_match('30_days')
    return state

# Wrap invocation
graph = StateGraph(...)  # your graph
result = vex.verify(graph.invoke)({"user_id": "123", "cart": [...]},
                   policy="ecommerce-workflow")

implementation.title: Add Vex as a Graph Node for Continuous Monitoring
implementation.description: Embed Vex checkpoints after parallel branches and before human-in-loop to catch merge conflicts.

implementation.title: Prevent Schema Drift with Vex State Validators
implementation.description: Validate checkpoint schema evolution to avoid deserialization errors after adding compliance fields.
implementation.code:
import vex
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
from typing import TypedDict

class CompliantCartState(TypedDict):
    cart_items: list
    price: float
    gdpr_consent: bool  # New field
    ada_compliant: bool

@vex.policy(name="cart-schema-v2")
def validate_cart_schema(state: CompliantCartState):
    vex.expect('gdpr_consent' in state).to_be(True, reason="GDPR consent required")
    vex.expect(state['ada_compliant']).to_be(True)
    vex.expect(len(state['cart_items']) > 0).to_be(True)

# Use with checkpointer
graph = StateGraph(CompliantCartState).compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver())

# Vex automatically validates on load
thread = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "cart-123"}}
result = graph.invoke({"action": "add_item"}, thread)
# Raises if old checkpoint lacks new compliance fields

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangGraph in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: FashionCo deployed LangGraph to power their intelligent shopping assistant, handling recommendation → pricing → checkout flows. The graph used parallel branches for personalized styling suggestions and dynamic pricing based on inventory and demand. Human-in-loop review was implemented for high-value carts (> $500), with conditional edges bypassing review for low-risk transactions. However, during Black Friday, cyclic execution in the pricing node caused runaway discounts (50% → 75% → 90%), while parallel recommendation branches corrupted state by overwriting customer loyalty discount codes with promotional ones.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex as a verification node after parallel merges and wrapping graph.invoke(), cyclic pricing was blocked at 75% discount with FTC substantiation checks. State corruption was prevented by validating discount code precedence, reducing refund disputes by 92%. Schema changes for GDPR consent fields were safely rolled out with Vex checkpoint validation, avoiding workflow crashes. The result: $2.3M in protected revenue and zero FTC complaints during peak season.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk langgraph` and protect your LangGraph workflows from compliance failures.
